headline: GSTR-3B Late Fees and Interest in India
h1: GSTR-3B Late Fees and Interest in India
title: GSTR-3B Late Fees and Interest in India
desc: Understand GSTR-3B late fees, interest rates, due dates, nil-return rules and cash-ledger payment under GST law.
lede: If GSTR-3B is filed after the due date, late fee applies for the delay and interest may apply on tax paid late or wrongly used input tax credit, as per Sections 39, 47 and 50 of the CGST Act and corresponding SGST or UTGST law. The GST portal generally computes both late fee and interest when the return is filed.
body:
When is GSTR-3B due?

GSTR-3B is normally due by the 20th day of the month following the tax period for monthly taxpayers, as per Section 39 of the CGST Act and GST return due-date rules. Quarterly taxpayers, including eligible taxpayers under the QRMP scheme, have notified quarterly due dates that may differ by state category. Because due dates can be extended by CBIC notifications, the GST portal date should be checked for the relevant tax period.

What late fee is charged for a late GSTR-3B?

A late GSTR-3B attracts late fee for each day of delay, subject to the notified cap, under Section 47 of the CGST Act and corresponding SGST or UTGST law. For GSTR-3B, CBIC Notification No. 49/2019-Central Tax dated 7 October 2019 and corresponding SGST notifications prescribe ₹50 per day under CGST and ₹50 per day under SGST for normal returns, subject to the notified maximum; for a nil return, the rate is ₹20 per day under each tax head, subject to the notified maximum. The GST portal shows the CGST and SGST or UTGST components separately and applies the cap for the relevant return period.

What counts as a nil GSTR-3B?

A nil GSTR-3B is not simply a return with no tax payable; under the late-fee notification, it generally means no outward supply, no reverse charge liability, no input tax credit, no tax payable and no refund claim. If any of these items is reported, the normal late-fee rate generally applies, subject to the portal computation.

How is interest calculated on late tax payment?

Interest is payable when tax remains unpaid or is short-paid beyond the due date, at 18% per annum, as per Section 50(1) of the CGST Act and CBIC Notification No. 13/2019-Central Tax dated 7 October 2019. Interest runs from the day after the due date until the date of payment. CBIC Circular No. 125/44/2019-GST dated 18 November 2019 clarifies that, for delayed tax payment, interest is applied to the tax paid through the electronic cash ledger; the GST portal computes this at filing. Wrongly availed or utilised input tax credit attracts interest at 24% per annum under Section 50(2) and CBIC Notification No. 14/2019-Central Tax.

Can late fee and interest be paid from ITC?

No. Late fee and interest must be paid from the electronic cash ledger, not from input tax credit. Input tax credit can be used for tax payable under Section 49(3) of the CGST Act, while the cash ledger is used for tax paid in cash, interest, late fee and other amounts under Section 49(4).

What should be checked before filing late?

Before filing a delayed GSTR-3B, reconcile the tax liability, cash payment and ITC utilisation, then review the late fee and interest shown by the GST portal. Rates, due dates and notifications can change, so the portal computation and current CBIC and State GST notifications should be confirmed for the tax period being filed.
faqs: What is the late fee for late GSTR-3B filing?

For GSTR-3B, CBIC Notification No. 49/2019-Central Tax dated 7 October 2019 provides ₹50 per day under CGST for normal returns and ₹20 per day under CGST for nil returns, with corresponding SGST or UTGST amounts and notified caps; confirm the GST portal computation for the total payable.
Is interest charged for late GSTR-3B filing?

Interest is charged when tax is paid late or short-paid, generally at 18% per annum under Section 50(1) of the CGST Act and CBIC Notification No. 13/2019-Central Tax; wrongly availed or utilised ITC attracts 24% per annum under Section 50(2) and CBIC Notification No. 14/2019-Central Tax.
Can I pay late fee and interest using ITC?

No. Late fee and interest are paid from the electronic cash ledger; input tax credit can be used for tax payable under Section 49(3) of the CGST Act, while Section 49(4) covers cash-ledger payments such as interest and late fee.
source: This article is general information, not advice. Tax rules and figures change — confirm current provisions with the official portals (incometax.gov.in / gst.gov.in) or contact us before acting.
